# Copilot Instructions for QuizletKiller

This repository is extremely small at the moment.  Most of the
"real" work lives in the planning document (`plan.md`); the code is
currently just a static HTML/CSS prototype.  The instructions below
focus on what an AI agent needs to know to be useful in this codebase
and how the project is meant to evolve.

---

## 🧠 Big‑Picture Vision

* **Goal**: "Destroy Quizlet for good" by building a friendly,
  powerful spaced‑repetition flashcard app.  See the roadmap in
  `plan.md` for details of the proposed architecture (React
  Native/Flutter frontend, FSRS scheduler, NoSQL backend, LLM‑powered
  content creation, collaborative study rooms, etc.).
* **Current state**: `index.html`, `css/style.css`, and `js/main.js`
  form a working flashcard app with three views (Study, List,
  Add/Manage).  There is no build system, framework, or server code.
* **Why structure exists**: the repo is a functional prototype of the
  future product.  Any code you add should connect back to the
  ideas in `plan.md` unless the user instructs otherwise.

## 📂 Repository Layout

```
/                ── root of the web prototype
  index.html     ─ main page with three views (Study / List / Add)
  css/style.css  ─ all styles for the app
  js/main.js     ─ all application logic (views, storage, drag/drop)
  plan.md        ─ developer/strategy notes (read frequently!)
  README.md      ─ brief project description
```

Treat `plan.md` as the primary source of truth when you need
context about why things are the way they are or what directions the
project might go next.

## ⚙️ Developer Workflows

* **Local development**: open `index.html` in a browser.  There is no
  npm/yarn install, build, or test command.  If you add tooling, also
  update this file with instructions.
* **Version control**: standard Git.  Keep commits small and
  descriptive; the repository is tiny so diffs should remain readable.
* **Testing**: none currently.  When adding JS or backend code, follow
  whatever test framework the user introduces and update this
  document accordingly.

## 🧩 Conventions & Patterns

* This codebase currently uses plain HTML and a single CSS file; do
  not assume any framework exists unless you add one explicitly.
* When modifying markup, use semantic tags and keep styles under
  `css/style.css`.  Avoid inline styles in `index.html` unless it's a
  quick prototype (existing inlined styles are minimal).
* If you create new directories (e.g. `js/`, `src/`), add a brief
  note here describing their purpose.
* The current flashcard prototype stores data in `localStorage`,
  including card groups and filter state.  See `js/main.js` for
  examples of persistence, filtering by text/group, and drag/drop
  reordering in the Manage view.  The app uses three views switched
  via a nav bar: Study (single-card flip), List (scrollable, click to
  jump), and Add/Manage (create, edit, delete, reorder).  Any time
  you extend storage or introduce a backend, update this guidance.

## 🔗 External Dependencies

None at the moment.  Future work may introduce:
1. Front‑end frameworks (React, Vue, etc.)
2. Build tools (Webpack, Vite, etc.)
3. Backend services or APIs (e.g. auth/data storage)

A common early choice is to lean on a managed backend like
[Supabase](https://supabase.com).  It provides:

* Email/password (and OAuth) authentication out-of-the-box
* A Postgres database with a simple REST interface and realtime
  updates
* Hosted storage for media files

Agents should assume that card data and user accounts would live in a
remote database once the prototype grows.  For now the codebase uses
`localStorage` as a stand-in.  When adding auth or persistence, update
this section with links to any newly added SDKs or environment
setup steps.

When new dependencies appear, update this file to describe their role.

## ❓ Guidance for AI Agents

1. **Read `plan.md` first.** It contains the strategy and feature set
   the human expects.  Use it to judge the appropriateness of any
   suggestion or change.
2. **Be conservative.** Right now, adding new code is mostly about
   fleshing out the prototype, not rewriting the architecture.  Ask
   clarifying questions if the scope of work isn’t obvious.
3. **Document your assumptions.** If you introduce a new file,
   directory, or dependency, mention it in both your commit message and
   here so future agents understand the change.


> _Need help?  Ask the user what direction the prototype should take or
> what vision from `plan.md` to prioritize._
